+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KRE1SSN9AX8R67XC58HHQKKB
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a980
+title: Relocate DynamicSources and friends out of swissarmyhammer-kanban
+---
+## What
+
+`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` and `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dynamic_sources.rs` host a cluster of types that are **not** kanban-specific. They were placed in the kanban crate when the command-scope work first landed because that's where `commands_for_scope` lived, but conceptually each belongs to its domain crate. Kanban becomes one consumer (and producer of `BoardInfo`) among several.
+
+### Current home ŌåÆ proposed home
+
+| Type | Today | Belongs in | Why |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| `DynamicSources` (aggregator) | `swissarmyhammer-kanban::scope_commands` | `swissarmyhammer-commands` | The consumer-agnostic command-scope concern; `commands_for_scope` already lives or should live here. Resolvers / pickers / view-kind filtering are all command-layer infrastructure. |
+| `ViewInfo` | `swissarmyhammer-kanban::scope_commands` | `swissarmyhammer-views` | Describes a view by id/name/kind/entity_type ŌĆö pure view-domain data. |
+| `PerspectiveInfo` | `swissarmyhammer-kanban::scope_commands` | `swissarmyhammer-perspectives` | Lightweight perspective descriptor ŌĆö the perspectives crate already owns the heavyweight `Perspective` type. |
+| `PerspectiveFieldInfo` | `swissarmyhammer-kanban::scope_commands` | `swissarmyhammer-perspectives` (or `swissarmyhammer-fields`) | Denormalized field-id + display-name pair for a perspective's columns. The display-name comes from `FieldsContext`, so either home is defensible; perspectives crate is closer to the consumer. |
+| `WindowInfo` | `swissarmyhammer-kanban::scope_commands` | `swissarmyhammer-commands` (or new ui-state) | GUI runtime descriptor that the scope dispatcher consumes; it has nothing to do with kanban specifically. |
+| `BoardInfo` | `swissarmyhammer-kanban::scope_commands` | **stays in kanban** | Boards are the only genuinely kanban-specific concept here. |
+| `PerspectiveFieldsResolver` | `swissarmyhammer-kanban::commands::options_resolvers` | `swissarmyhammer-perspectives` | Builds options from a perspective's field list ŌĆö perspective-domain logic. |
+| `ViewKindsResolver` | `swissarmyhammer-kanban::commands::options_resolvers` | `swissarmyhammer-views` | Constant list of `ViewKind` variants via `as_kebab_str()` ŌĆö pure view-domain. |
+| `SortDirectionsResolver` | `swissarmyhammer-kanban::commands::options_resolvers` | `swissarmyhammer-commands` | Two static entries (`asc`, `desc`); no domain dependency. |
+| `default_options_registry()` | kanban | refactored ŌĆö each domain crate registers its own resolvers; kanban only registers its kanban-specific ones (none, currently) | The dependency direction is the test: kanban knows about views/perspectives, not the other way around. |
+| `commands_for_scope`, `filter_by_view_kind`, `enrich_options` | `swissarmyhammer-kanban::scope_commands` | `swissarmyhammer-commands` | Already consumer-agnostic in spirit (uses `&dyn Any` for option-resolver source data); the implementation just hasn't moved yet. |
+| `build_dynamic_sources` + `DynamicSourcesInputs` | `swissarmyhammer-kanban::dynamic_sources` | split: aggregator stays where it can see all the crates (probably kanban-app or a thin assembly crate); per-domain `gather_*` helpers move to their respective crates | The aggregator is necessarily cross-domain, but the kanban crate is the wrong host because it doesn't know about views or perspectives ergonomically. |
+
+### The "kanban knows everything" anti-pattern
+
+Today `swissarmyhammer-kanban` depends on `swissarmyhammer-views`, `swissarmyhammer-perspectives`, `swissarmyhammer-fields`, etc. AND owns the command-scope concept that knits them together. That makes kanban a god-crate. Inverting:
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-commands` defines the scoping concept, `CommandDef`, `OptionsResolver`, `OptionsRegistry`, `commands_for_scope`, the aggregator `DynamicSources`, and the per-domain trait that lets each domain crate contribute a `gather_*` implementation.
+- Each domain crate (`-views`, `-perspectives`, etc.) depends on `-commands` (which is small and stable), implements its own `gather` function, and registers its own resolvers via a small `register_resolvers(&mut OptionsRegistry)` entrypoint.
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban` does the same for `BoardInfo` and any future kanban-only commands. It is no longer the central hub.
+
+### Migration strategy
+
+1. **Add the new crates / module locations** (no moves yet). E.g. add `pub struct ViewInfo {ŌĆ”}` in `swissarmyhammer-views`, leave the kanban definition in place but mark it `#[deprecated]` with a `pub use swissarmyhammer_views::ViewInfo` re-export.
+2. **Migrate one type at a time**, starting with the leafiest (`BoardInfo` stays; `WindowInfo`, `ViewInfo`, `PerspectiveFieldInfo`, `PerspectiveInfo` move one per commit). Each move:
+   - Define in target crate.
+   - Add `pub use` re-export from old location (so existing call sites compile).
+   - Find every direct construction of the type (`{ ... }` literal); update the `use` to point at the new home; remove the re-export.
+   - Compile, test, commit.
+3. **Migrate the resolvers** the same way: define in the domain crate, register from the domain crate, kanban no longer owns the registration.
+4. **Migrate `DynamicSources`** last ŌĆö it touches every other type. Move the aggregator struct to `swissarmyhammer-commands`; leave a re-export until callers update.
+5. **Migrate `commands_for_scope`** + `filter_by_view_kind` + `enrich_options` to `swissarmyhammer-commands`. The Rust-side `OptionsContext::data: &dyn Any` already supports this ŌĆö kanban-side resolvers downcast to whatever data they need.
+6. **Migrate `build_dynamic_sources` + `DynamicSourcesInputs`** ŌĆö split into per-domain `gather_views`, `gather_perspectives`, `gather_boards`, with the aggregator in a thin assembly crate (or `kanban-app` itself, which is already the assembly point for the GUI).
+
+### Out of scope
+
+- Any change to the wire format of `CommandDef` / `ParamDef` / `OptionsResolver` traits. Those are stable from the prior tasks.
+- The frontend `<CommandButton>` / `<CommandPopover>` work ŌĆö sees only the YAML-shape `CommandDef` and is unaffected by where the Rust types live.
+- Building a new "ui-state" crate for `WindowInfo` unless one already feels obvious ŌĆö staging into `swissarmyhammer-commands` for now is fine.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `ViewInfo` is defined in `swissarmyhammer-views`. `swissarmyhammer-kanban` does not define it; its old definition is gone (not deprecated ŌĆö fully removed).
+- [ ] `PerspectiveInfo` and `PerspectiveFieldInfo` are defined in `swissarmyhammer-perspectives` (or `swissarmyhammer-fields`, document the choice). Kanban does not define either.
+- [ ] `WindowInfo` is defined in `swissarmyhammer-commands` (or whatever single canonical command-layer location). Kanban does not define it.
+- [ ] `DynamicSources` (the aggregator) is defined in `swissarmyhammer-commands`. Kanban does not define it.
+- [ ] `BoardInfo` remains in `swissarmyhammer-kanban` ŌĆö it's the only genuinely kanban-specific descriptor.
+- [ ] `commands_for_scope`, `filter_by_view_kind`, and `enrich_options` live in `swissarmyhammer-commands`. Kanban consumes them like any other domain crate.
+- [ ] `PerspectiveFieldsResolver` is defined and registered from `swissarmyhammer-perspectives`. `ViewKindsResolver` is defined and registered from `swissarmyhammer-views`. `SortDirectionsResolver` stays in `swissarmyhammer-commands`. The kanban crate has zero `OptionsResolver` registrations after this task ŌĆö boards have no enum-shaped params today.
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-kanban`'s `dependencies` in `Cargo.toml` stay the same shape (it still depends on views, perspectives, fields, commands), BUT no symbols are re-exported from kanban as a passthrough ŌĆö every consumer imports from the canonical crate.
+- [ ] All existing tests still pass: `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` is green.
+- [ ] No `pub use ... as ...` legacy re-exports left behind. If a path moved, every caller imports from the new path.
+- [ ] `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` clean; `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Move corresponds 1:1 ŌĆö every test that previously exercised `ViewInfo`/`PerspectiveInfo`/etc. still exercises the same behavior at the new home. Specifically:
+  - `swissarmyhammer-views`: gains a test that confirms `ViewInfo` constructs and serializes as expected (move the relevant test from `scope_commands.rs`).
+  - `swissarmyhammer-perspectives`: gains tests for `PerspectiveInfo` + `PerspectiveFieldInfo` constructors and for the `PerspectiveFieldsResolver` (the existing resolver tests move with the resolver).
+  - `swissarmyhammer-commands`: gains tests for `DynamicSources` construction, `commands_for_scope` filtering, `filter_by_view_kind`, `enrich_options`. Move the integration tests from `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/options_enrichment.rs` here (or leave them in kanban as cross-crate integration tests ŌĆö document the choice).
+  - `swissarmyhammer-kanban`: loses tests for types that moved; gains nothing new (kanban no longer has standalone command-scope responsibilities). The `dynamic_sources_headless.rs` integration test changes shape ŌĆö it now calls the assembly entrypoint which itself calls each domain's `gather_*`.
+- [ ] A cross-crate integration test (probably in `swissarmyhammer-commands/tests/`) confirms a `DynamicSources` aggregated from views, perspectives, and boards emits the same `commands_for_scope` result as today.
+- [ ] Run: `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` ŌĆö green.
+- [ ] Run: `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ŌĆö clean.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- This task is mechanical but large. Do it incrementally ŌĆö one type per commit so the diff is reviewable. The migration strategy section above is the suggested commit sequence.
+- After each move, run `cargo check --workspace` before moving on. A broken intermediate state is much worse than ten small commits.
+- Update the migration tasks (`01KRE1YA65MMG29RDQDQ0VPJQG` Filter, `01KRE1ZTYJ5PPTQ29K72KE88B5` Group, `01KRE21GJMPP289N1HSTMJG5HE` Add + Sort) to reference the new crate locations once this task starts touching their target files. They have annotations that point at `swissarmyhammer-kanban/...` paths today; those need to point at `swissarmyhammer-commands` / `swissarmyhammer-perspectives` / `swissarmyhammer-views` after this task lands.
+- The new `<CommandButton>` / `<CommandPopover>` task (`01KRE1VDTC4MNKN3YPR619NDQK`) is unaffected ŌĆö it only sees the YAML-shape `CommandDef` over the Tauri bridge. It does not care where the Rust types live.
+- This task is best sequenced AFTER `01KRE1VDTC4MNKN3YPR619NDQK` (so the frontend foundation lands without conflict) but BEFORE the three command-migration tasks (5ŌĆō7), so those migrations reference the post-refactor homes from the start. #command-driven-ui

+## Implementation Note: Architectural Contradiction Surfaced 2026-05-12
+
+Halted at the "research before writing" step of `/implement` because the task description contains an unresolved architectural contradiction. Per the user's invocation prompt instructions, I am surfacing the concern as an implementation note rather than carrying out a structural change that wasn't on the task.
+
+### The conflict
+
+The acceptance criteria mandate, simultaneously:
+
+1. `DynamicSources` (the aggregator) is defined in `swissarmyhammer-commands`.
+2. `BoardInfo` remains in `swissarmyhammer-kanban`.
+3. `ViewInfo` is defined in `swissarmyhammer-views`.
+4. `PerspectiveInfo` / `PerspectiveFieldInfo` are defined in `swissarmyhammer-perspectives`.
+
+`DynamicSources` is a concrete struct with strongly-typed fields:
+
+```rust
+pub struct DynamicSources {
+    pub views: Vec<ViewInfo>,
+    pub boards: Vec<BoardInfo>,
+    pub windows: Vec<WindowInfo>,
+    pub perspectives: Vec<PerspectiveInfo>,
+}
+```
+
+If `DynamicSources` lives in `swissarmyhammer-commands`, then `swissarmyhammer-commands` must depend on `swissarmyhammer-views` (ViewInfo), `swissarmyhammer-perspectives` (PerspectiveInfo), and `swissarmyhammer-kanban` (BoardInfo). The `-kanban` dependency creates a cycle because `-kanban` already depends on `-commands` ŌĆö the workspace will not build.
+
+The task's own architecture-rationale section says "Each domain crate (`-views`, `-perspectives`, etc.) depends on `-commands` (which is small and stable), implements its own `gather` function". That establishes `-commands` as the small stable base and the explicit anti-pattern is to add domain-crate deps to it. The user's invocation prompt was explicit: "If you discover the workspace dependency graph would require adding a new dep edge ... stop and surface the concern as an implementation note rather than carrying out a structural change that wasn't on the task."
+
+The contradiction is also visible inside the task body itself. Migration step 6 says: "Migrate `build_dynamic_sources` + `DynamicSourcesInputs` ŌĆö split into per-domain `gather_views`, `gather_perspectives`, `gather_boards`, with the aggregator in a **thin assembly crate (or `kanban-app` itself, which is already the assembly point for the GUI)**." That contradicts the acceptance criterion that `DynamicSources` lives in `-commands`.
+
+Note that `commands_for_scope` itself can move to `-commands` without trouble, because `OptionsContext.data: &dyn Any` already type-erases the `DynamicSources` reference. The `-commands` crate never needs `DynamicSources` by type to host `commands_for_scope` ŌĆö it only needs it by `&dyn Any`.
+
+### Resolution options (need owner decision)
+
+**Option A** ŌĆö relax acceptance criterion #1 (recommended): keep `DynamicSources` in a higher-level assembly crate (`kanban-app`) rather than `-commands`. `commands_for_scope` still moves to `-commands` and consumes the aggregator via `&dyn Any`. All other acceptance criteria (ViewInfo / PerspectiveInfo / BoardInfo / WindowInfo / SortDirectionsResolver homes) stay as written. This matches migration step 6's hint and the type-erasure design from the prior task (01KRE1SSN9AX8R67XC58HHQKKB).
+
+**Option B** ŌĆö relax acceptance criterion #2: move `BoardInfo` and every other *Info type into `-commands` (they are all lightweight wire descriptors with no domain logic). Then `DynamicSources` plus all its fields live in `-commands` with zero new domain-crate deps. This contradicts the table row that calls BoardInfo "the only genuinely kanban-specific descriptor".
+
+**Option C** ŌĆö type-erase the fields: change `DynamicSources` to `Vec<Box<dyn Any>>` keyed maps. Compiles, but breaks every resolver's strongly-typed access pattern (`dyn_src.perspectives.iter().find(|p| p.id == ...)`) and is a much larger churn than the task envisions.
+
+**My recommendation: Option A.** It is the only option consistent with both the task's stated architectural direction ("`-commands` is the small stable base") and the existing `&dyn Any` design from the prior task. The acceptance criterion stating `DynamicSources` lives in `-commands` appears to be a description error that contradicts the rest of the task. Migration step 6 already hints at the right answer.
+
+Nothing else has been touched ŌĆö no code or Cargo.toml has been modified. Task moved back to `todo`. Awaiting owner clarification on which acceptance criterion to relax before re-entering implementation.
+
+---
+
 ## What

 `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` and `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dynamic_sources.rs` host a cluster of types that are **not** kanban-specific. They were placed in the kanban crate when the command-scope work first landed because that's where `commands_for_scope` lived, but conceptually each belongs to its domain crate. Kanban becomes one consumer (and producer of `BoardInfo`) among several.

 ### The "kanban knows everything" anti-pattern

 Today `swissarmyhammer-kanban` depends on `swissarmyhammer-views`, `swissarmyhammer-perspectives`, `swissarmyhammer-fields`, etc. AND owns the command-scope concept that knits them together. That makes kanban a god-crate. Inverting:

-- `swissarmyhammer-commands` defines the scoping concept, `CommandDef`, `OptionsResolver`, `OptionsRegistry`, `commands_for_scope`, the aggregator `DynamicSources`, and the per-domain trait that lets each domain crate contribute a `gather_*` implementation.
-- Each domain crate (`-views`, `-perspectives`, etc.) depends on `-commands` (which is small and stable), implements its own `gather` function, and registers its own resolvers via a small `register_resolvers(&mut OptionsRegistry)` entrypoint.
-- `swissarmyhammer-kanban` does the same for `BoardInfo` and any future kanban-only commands. It is no longer the central hub.
+- `swissarmyhammer-commands` defines the scoping concept, `CommandDef`, `OptionsResolver`, `OptionsRegistry`, `commands_for_scope`, `WindowInfo`, `SortDirectionsResolver`. It stays the small stable base ŌĆö no new domain-crate deps.
+- Each domain crate (`-views`, `-perspectives`, etc.) depends on `-commands`, defines its own `*Info` types and `gather_*` helper, and registers its own resolvers.
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban` does the same for `BoardInfo` and its `gather_boards` helper.
+- `kanban-app` is the assembly point: it depends on every domain crate plus `-commands`, defines `DynamicSources`, calls each domain's `gather_*`, and hands the aggregator to `commands_for_scope` via `&dyn Any`.

-To let per-domain resolvers (e.g. `PerspectiveFieldsResolver` in perspectives) coexist without each depending on the consumer's aggregator type (`DynamicSources` in kanban), commands now exports an `OptionsSources` typed multimap. The consumer composes one per `commands_for_scope` call, inserts per-domain data structs (`PerspectivesOptionsData`, etc.), and threads `&sources as &dyn Any` into `OptionsContext`. Each per-domain resolver downcasts to `&OptionsSources` then calls `OptionsSources::get::<DomainOptionsData>()`. This keeps the `OptionsResolver` trait surface unchanged (the explicit out-of-scope item) while letting the per-domain resolvers live in their domain crates.
